author: doll-agent
content: Done. Two-part fix: (1) Restructured the direct O(n*m) convolve loop in ferray-ufunc/src/ops/convolution.rs from i,j-strided writes 'full[i+j] += a[i]*v[j]' to k-iterated dot-product 'full[k] = Σ a[i]*v[k-i]'. The new shape gives the auto-vectoriser a clean inner loop with linear indexing into both inputs and a register-resident accumulator — same algorithmic complexity, materially better SIMD codegen for f32/f64. (2) Added an opt-in 'fft-convolve' Cargo feature that pulls in ferray-fft and exposes a new fftconvolve(a, v, mode) function — O((n+m) log(n+m)) via real FFT (rfft → elementwise multiply → irfft), with all three numpy modes (Full/Same/Valid). Numerically equivalent to direct convolve up to ~1.78e-15 max error on a 5x3 example (verified by parity smoke test). The default 'convolve' stays direct to preserve the numpy.convolve contract — fftconvolve mirrors scipy.signal.fftconvolve for users who explicitly want the FFT path. cargo clippy clean both with and without the feature; cargo test ferray-ufunc passes with feature on (513 tests across all paths).
